findings.evidence: The new file has `variable {Ω α} [MeasurableSpace Ω] [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [MeasurableSpace α]` at line 39 and `directingMeasure` is `condExpKernel μ (tailProcess X)` at lines 45-46. Mathlib’s `condExpKernel` itself requires `[StandardBorelSpace Ω]` (`Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/Condexp.lean:61-72`). The roadmap states the standard-Borel hypothesis belongs on `α`, keeps `Ω` only measurable, and says an extra `[StandardBorelSpace Ω]` from `condExpKernel` is internal and should not appear in the public statement (`TauCetiRoadmap/Exchangeability/README.md:69-77`).
findings.fix: Define the directing measure via the tail-conditional path/value law, e.g. `condDistrib` on `ℕ → α` or directly on `α`, with `[StandardBorelSpace α]` and only `[MeasurableSpace Ω]`; keep any `condExpKernel`/identity-kernel use internal if needed.
findings.issue: `directingMeasure` and all its API require `[StandardBorelSpace Ω]`, narrowing the public de Finetti directing-measure construction to standard-Borel sample spaces instead of arbitrary measurable Ω with standard-Borel value space α.

summary: The construction proves the advertised conditional-law identity, but it formalizes the public directing measure through an identity-valued conditional kernel on Ω, imposing the wrong standard-Borel hypothesis for the de Finetti target.
